items.content: Inventory ~/labs/jcode/docs/**. For each file classify as {documentation | PM/tracking | stale}. Note code/CI references to each doc path (grep the repo). archive/ is historical: note it, leave it. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. Surface contract is law: PM/tracking -> notes (~/notes/projects/jcode); documentation -> repo docs. Findings/analysis docs stay in repo even if they list open items. READ-ONLY: no writes, no commits. DONE-CONDITION: every docs/ file appears exactly once in your table.

Inventory ~/notes/projects/jcode/**. Classify each file as {documentation | PM/tracking | stale}. Also list duplicated/contradictory content vs ~/labs/jcode/docs (name the docs file and the overlapping facts). OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale} plus duplication/conflict list. Surface contract is law: PM/tracking -> notes; documentation -> repo docs. READ-ONLY: no writes, no commits. DONE-CONDITION: every notes file appears exactly once in your table.

From survey-docs and survey-notes outputs, produce the reconciliation map: (a) PM-in-docs files that move to notes, (b) duplicated facts that collapse to one home (state the winner), (c) stale files to git rm (archive/ stays). Conflict rule: git/code reality > notes > docs; record each resolution. OUTPUT: one table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}, every surveyed file exactly once. STOP after producing the map: report it and DO NOT write, move, or commit anything. This is a human review gate.

Execute migrate-PM per the approved merge-map: move each PM/tracking file from ~/labs/jcode/docs into ~/notes/projects/jcode (land in notes with front-matter and a provenance comment naming original repo path+commit, then git rm from repo). No verbatim duplication between surfaces. A pre-commit hook BLOCKS adding PM files to docs/; do not fight it. Never git add -A: pathspec-scope every commit, conventional commit messages, commit in both repos as you go. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every mapped migrate file handled exactly once.

Execute dedup per the approved merge-map: within ~/notes/projects/jcode collapse duplicated/contradictory facts to one authoritative statement. Conflict rule: git/code reality > notes > docs; record each resolution. Fix links afterward. Never copy verbatim between surfaces. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits in ~/notes as you go. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every dedup item handled exactly once.

Execute prune per the approved merge-map: git rm each stale doc in ~/labs/jcode/docs marked delete (git preserves history). Leave docs/archive/ alone. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits in ~/labs/jcode as you go, never git add -A. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every delete item handled exactly once.

Independent verification: (1) no PM/tracking left in ~/labs/jcode/docs (run the repo pre-commit heuristic over all docs/ files; expect zero hits); (2) no dangling markdown links either direction between docs/ and ~/notes/projects/jcode; (3) ~/notes/projects/jcode/project.md index is current (all files indexed, no ghost entries). Fix trivial breakage (links, index lines) with pathspec-scoped conventional commits; report structural issues back. OUTPUT: table {check, result, evidence}. DONE-CONDITION: all three checks pass or are reported failing with evidence.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Per ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/ci-green-and-fork-issues.md section 2: run scripts/dev_cargo.sh clippy --fix on the workspace, review the diff, then hand-fix the leftovers: dead_code platform-gated FFI consts (proc_pidfd*, KILLALL_PROCESS_NAME, RELOAD_HANDOFF_EVENT_POLL_MS) with #[cfg(...)] or narrow #[allow(dead_code)] with a comment, plus any remaining needless_return/collapsible_if/needless_lifetimes/manual_is_multiple_of/needless_range_loop/unnecessary-unsafe. CRITICAL: cargo only via nix develop --command, NEVER two nix develop invocations in parallel (shellHook deadlocks); run everything sequentially. Validate with scripts/fork-touched-clippy.sh (expect zero or near-zero lints). Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (chore(clippy): ...), never git add -A. OUTPUT: lint counts before/after and commit hashes.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Implement the P0 marker lifecycle fix from ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/audit-state-ttl-2026-07-14.md rows 2-4, following the EXISTING design in docs/proposals/swarm-lifecycle-remediation.md Issue #2 (read it; do not re-derive). Scope: (1) unconditional startup + periodic liveness sweep that DELETES dead-PID marker files in ~/.jcode/active_pids and ~/.jcode/streaming_pids (delete, not relabel; today reconcile skips markers whose session JSON cannot load - the sweep must not); (2) telemetry_active_sessions: write the PID into the marker file, sweep by PID liveness instead of 24h mtime, and de-duplicate the two path helper implementations (jcode-telemetry-core/src/lifecycle.rs vs jcode-app-core/src/telemetry_state.rs); (3) unit tests for the sweep (dead pid deleted, live pid kept, unreadable marker deleted). Key files: crates/jcode-storage/src/active_pids.rs, crates/jcode-base/src/session.rs (reconcile_active_sessions), crates/jcode-app-core/src/telemetry_state.rs, jcode-telemetry-core/src/lifecycle.rs, server background task registration. Validate: nix develop --command cargo test -p jcode-storage -p jcode-base -p jcode-app-core (sequential, never parallel nix develop). Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(state): ...). Evidence of success on this machine: ~/.jcode/active_pids had 29 stale and telemetry_active_sessions 1297 stale at audit time - after your change a server restart should sweep them; you may verify counts read-only but do NOT restart the shared server yourself.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Implement the P1 retention fixes from ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/audit-state-ttl-2026-07-14.md rows 7-10 and 22: (1) builds/: retention policy keeping binaries referenced by current/stable/shared-server/canary plus last N=5 versions; delete unreferenced older version dirs and stale migration contexts (crates/jcode-build-support/src/storage_helpers.rs); (2) logs/: rolling retention for general logs (non runtime-memory), e.g. delete *.log/*.jsonl older than 14 days, and a per-file size cap or rotation for build.log (truncate-on-open past cap is fine); (3) reload-traces/: keep newest 30 files or 14 days; (4) wire the sweeps into the existing server background maintenance task where runtime-memory-log pruning already runs (crates/jcode-app-core/src/server.rs ~1383). Unit tests for each retention function (fixture dirs with old/new files). Do NOT touch sessions/, auth/config files, or user-authored artifacts. Validate: nix develop --command cargo test -p jcode-build-support -p jcode-app-core (sequential). Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(storage): ...). Live context: builds/ is 1.9GB and logs/ 628MB on this machine.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Address the CRITICAL finding in ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/audit-orthogonality-2026-07-14.md: JCODE_DEBUG_CONTROL couples debug-socket exposure with safety-relevant authorization, reachable via config, env, AND a file toggle (crates/jcode-app-core/src/server/util.rs:10-28, debug_command_exec.rs:80). Design first, then implement: (1) READ the audit section and the fork-bomb learning in ~/notes/projects/jcode/project.md learning 2; (2) split concerns so that no single toggle both exposes the debug control surface and alters any safety mechanism (timeouts, kill paths, caps, guards) - one flag = one job; (3) the file toggle must be explicit, announced in logs when it activates, and covered by a test; (4) keep backward compatibility where safe: if the legacy combined semantics must change behavior, prefer the SAFE default and document the migration in the commit message. Also fix the two cheap Medium findings if trivial: JCODE_OPENROUTER_ALLOW_NO_AUTH double-duty (auth relaxation should not mark a profile configured for auth-status reporting) - skip if nontrivial. Validate: nix develop --command cargo test -p jcode-app-core -p jcode-base (sequential). Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(safety): ...). OUTPUT: before/after semantics table for each touched toggle.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Implement the sharpest recommendation from ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/audit-self-managers-2026-07-14.md: (1) self-manager #6 (source-checkout auto-updater: git pull + cargo build + exec from startup, src/cli/startup.rs:300-337, src/cli/hot_exec.rs:217-331): remove the default auto-run; require explicit opt-in (config key or explicit `jcode update` / selfdev command). Startup may still CHECK and announce that an update is available. (2) self-managers #3/#4 (newer-binary auto-reload + reload marker handoff): add the announce/record step - a structured log line (and reload-trace entry where the plumbing already exists) stating what initiated the reload (source: auto-update / config-change / selfdev / debug) BEFORE it happens. Keep behavior otherwise. Tests: opt-out default verified (no auto-run without the config key), reload source recorded. Validate: nix develop --command cargo test -p jcode-app-core plus a targeted src/cli test if present (sequential). Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(selfmgmt): ...).

Independent verification in ~/labs/jcode of the hygiene-fix chain: (1) nix develop --command cargo test --workspace green (sequential, single nix develop); (2) scripts/fork-touched-clippy.sh green; (3) marker sweep: unit tests exist and pass; grep confirms deletion-based sweep is wired into startup/background maintenance; (4) retention: unit tests exist for builds/logs/reload-traces retention and the sweeps are registered in the background task; (5) JCODE_DEBUG_CONTROL: confirm no single toggle both exposes debug control and alters a safety mechanism (read the new code; cite lines); (6) auto-updater: confirm default startup path cannot git-pull/build/exec without explicit opt-in (cite lines). Fix only trivial breakage with scoped commits; report anything structural. Then update ~/notes/projects/jcode: check off the corresponding boxes in project.md learnings 1-3 implementation items and add a short status line to each audit file header; commit in ~/notes. OUTPUT: table {check, result, evidence}.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Implement the confirmed compaction watermark fix. READ FIRST: /tmp/jcode-audits/compaction-bug.md (full root-cause analysis with file:line citations, failing-test outline, 5-point fix proposal) and the root-cause section of ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/bug-openai-compaction-stall.md. Defect: crates/jcode-base/src/compaction.rs check_and_apply_compaction_with (~L1269) and hard_compact_with (~L1594) overwrite covers_up_to_turn/original_turn_count with the batch-local cutoff instead of accumulating; build_transfer_compaction_state (~L1844) shows the correct cumulative pattern. Implement proposal points 1-5: (1) cumulative covers_up_to_turn in check_and_apply_compaction_with; (2) same in hard_compact_with; (3) warn-log invariant guard when covers_up_to_turn != compacted_count after any compaction-apply, consistent with clamp_compacted_count_to_messages style; (4) consolidate the three Summary-construction sites into one helper (e.g. Summary::advance) so the arithmetic exists once; (5) regression tests: the two-round hard-compact test from the outline (must fail before, pass after), a force_compact_with/background second-round variant, and native-then-manual (apply_openai_native_compaction baseline followed by jcode-side compaction) pinning the camel scenario. TDD: commit or at least run the failing test BEFORE the fix to prove it fails. NOTE: crates/jcode-base/src/compaction_tests.rs may have leftover uncommitted scratch from the investigation agent; inspect git diff first and discard or subsume it deliberately. cargo ONLY via nix develop --command, sequential, never parallel nix develop. Validate: cargo test -p jcode-base -p jcode-compaction-core. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(compaction): ...). OUTPUT: failing-then-passing test evidence + commit hashes.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Two related daemon-degradation bugs found live 2026-07-14 (daemon at ~100% CPU, 441MB daily log): (A) Hot paths call Config::load() DIRECTLY instead of the cached config() accessor: crates/jcode-base/src/provider/account_failover.rs:same_provider_account_failover_enabled (called per completion attempt) and other direct Config::load() call sites outside config.rs (grep 'Config::load()' across crates/ and src/, excluding the cache in config.rs and tests). Each call re-parses TOML and re-logs EVERY unknown config key ('Unknown config key ... ignored' logged 1.2M times today at ~250/sec). Fix: (1) route hot-path callers through config(); (2) make unknown-key warnings fire once per config fingerprint (WarnOnce/should_fire pattern already exists in config_file.rs LOGGED_CONFIG_LAYER_SUMMARY), not per parse. (B) Sidecar model-override failure spam: crates/jcode-base/src/sidecar.rs backend_for_configured_model logs an ERROR on every Sidecar::new() when the configured provider (e.g. omlx:Qwen3.6-MoE) has no live route - ~874 errors per 1000 log lines observed. Fix: log the diagnostic once per (model, failure-kind) per process (or per config fingerprint), and keep the fallback behavior; do NOT cache the fallback decision itself since llm_backend_available is deliberately re-evaluated live. Add tests: unknown-key warning fires once across two loads with same fingerprint; sidecar fallback diagnostic deduped. cargo ONLY via nix develop --command, sequential, never parallel nix develop. Validate: cargo test -p jcode-base. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(perf): ... / fix(logging): ...). OUTPUT: before/after description + commit hashes.

items.content: Inventory ~/labs/jcode/docs/**. For each file classify as {documentation | PM/tracking | stale}. Note code/CI references to each doc path (grep the repo). archive/ is historical: note it, leave it. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. Surface contract is law: PM/tracking -> notes (~/notes/projects/jcode); documentation -> repo docs. Findings/analysis docs stay in repo even if they list open items. READ-ONLY: no writes, no commits. DONE-CONDITION: every docs/ file appears exactly once in your table.

Inventory ~/notes/projects/jcode/**. Classify each file as {documentation | PM/tracking | stale}. Also list duplicated/contradictory content vs ~/labs/jcode/docs (name the docs file and the overlapping facts). OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale} plus duplication/conflict list. Surface contract is law: PM/tracking -> notes; documentation -> repo docs. READ-ONLY: no writes, no commits. DONE-CONDITION: every notes file appears exactly once in your table.

From survey-docs and survey-notes outputs, produce the reconciliation map: (a) PM-in-docs files that move to notes, (b) duplicated facts that collapse to one home (state the winner), (c) stale files to git rm (archive/ stays). Conflict rule: git/code reality > notes > docs; record each resolution. OUTPUT: one table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}, every surveyed file exactly once. STOP after producing the map: report it and DO NOT write, move, or commit anything. This is a human review gate.

Execute migrate-PM per the approved merge-map: move each PM/tracking file from ~/labs/jcode/docs into ~/notes/projects/jcode (land in notes with front-matter and a provenance comment naming original repo path+commit, then git rm from repo). No verbatim duplication between surfaces. A pre-commit hook BLOCKS adding PM files to docs/; do not fight it. Never git add -A: pathspec-scope every commit, conventional commit messages, commit in both repos as you go. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every mapped migrate file handled exactly once.

Execute dedup per the approved merge-map: within ~/notes/projects/jcode collapse duplicated/contradictory facts to one authoritative statement. Conflict rule: git/code reality > notes > docs; record each resolution. Fix links afterward. Never copy verbatim between surfaces. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits in ~/notes as you go. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every dedup item handled exactly once.

Execute prune per the approved merge-map: git rm each stale doc in ~/labs/jcode/docs marked delete (git preserves history). Leave docs/archive/ alone. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits in ~/labs/jcode as you go, never git add -A. OUTPUT: table {file, class, target, action, one-line rationale}. DONE-CONDITION: every delete item handled exactly once.

Independent verification: (1) no PM/tracking left in ~/labs/jcode/docs (run the repo pre-commit heuristic over all docs/ files; expect zero hits); (2) no dangling markdown links either direction between docs/ and ~/notes/projects/jcode; (3) ~/notes/projects/jcode/project.md index is current (all files indexed, no ghost entries). Fix trivial breakage (links, index lines) with pathspec-scoped conventional commits; report structural issues back. OUTPUT: table {check, result, evidence}. DONE-CONDITION: all three checks pass or are reported failing with evidence.

Per ~/notes/projects/jcode/maintenance/ci-green-and-fork-issues.md section 2: run scripts/dev_cargo.sh clippy --fix on the workspace, review the diff, then hand-fix the leftovers: dead_code platform-gated FFI consts (proc_pidfd*, KILLALL_PROCESS_NAME, RELOAD_HANDOFF_EVENT_POLL_MS) with #[cfg(...)] or narrow #[allow(dead_code)] with a comment, plus any remaining needless_return/collapsible_if/needless_lifetimes/manual_is_multiple_of/needless_range_loop/unnecessary-unsafe. Defect: crates/jcode-base/src/compaction.rs check_and_apply_compaction_with (~L1269) and hard_compact_with (~L1594) overwrite covers_up_to_turn/original_turn_count with the batch-local cutoff instead of accumulating; build_transfer_compaction_state (~L1844) shows the correct cumulative pattern. Implement proposal points 1-5: (1) cumulative covers_up_to_turn in check_and_apply_compaction_with; (2) same in hard_compact_with; (3) warn-log invariant guard when covers_up_to_turn != compacted_count after any compaction-apply, consistent with clamp_compacted_count_to_messages style; (4) consolidate the three Summary-construction sites into one helper (e.g. Summary::advance) so the arithmetic exists once; (5) regression tests: the two-round hard-compact test from the outline (must fail before, pass after), a force_compact_with/background second-round variant, and native-then-manual (apply_openai_native_compaction baseline followed by jcode-side compaction) pinning the camel scenario. TDD: commit or at least run the failing test BEFORE the fix to prove it fails. NOTE: crates/jcode-base/src/compaction_tests.rs may have leftover uncommitted scratch from the investigation agent; inspect git diff first and discard or subsume it deliberately. cargo ONLY via nix develop --command, sequential, never parallel nix develop. Validate: cargo test -p jcode-base -p jcode-compaction-core. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(compaction): ...). OUTPUT: failing-then-passing test evidence + commit hashes.

Repo ~/labs/jcode, branch main. Two related daemon-degradation bugs found live 2026-07-14 (daemon at ~100% CPU, 441MB daily log): (A) Hot paths call Config::load() DIRECTLY instead of the cached config() accessor: crates/jcode-base/src/provider/account_failover.rs:same_provider_account_failover_enabled (called per completion attempt) and other direct Config::load() call sites outside config.rs (grep 'Config::load()' across crates/ and src/, excluding the cache in config.rs and tests). Each call re-parses TOML and re-logs EVERY unknown config key ('Unknown config key ... ignored' logged 1.2M times today at ~250/sec). Fix: (1) route hot-path callers through config(); (2) make unknown-key warnings fire once per config fingerprint (WarnOnce/should_fire pattern already exists in config_file.rs LOGGED_CONFIG_LAYER_SUMMARY), not per parse. (B) Sidecar model-override failure spam: crates/jcode-base/src/sidecar.rs backend_for_configured_model logs an ERROR on every Sidecar::new() when the configured provider (e.g. omlx:Qwen3.6-MoE) has no live route - ~874 errors per 1000 log lines observed. Fix: log the diagnostic once per (model, failure-kind) per process (or per config fingerprint), and keep the fallback behavior; do NOT cache the fallback decision itself since llm_backend_available is deliberately re-evaluated live. Add tests: unknown-key warning fires once across two loads with same fingerprint; sidecar fallback diagnostic deduped. cargo ONLY via nix develop --command, sequential, never parallel nix develop. Validate: cargo test -p jcode-base. Pathspec-scoped conventional commits (fix(perf): ... / fix(logging): ...). OUTPUT: before/after description + commit hashes.
